title: Anthropic Just Became a $380 Billion Unicorn—And Nobody Can Agree If It's Worth It
description: Anthropic closed a [$30 billion funding round](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) at a [$380 billion valuation](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) on February 12, 2026—the second-largest private tech raise ever. Now the real question: Is the AI startup's [$14 billion annualized revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) enough to justify a valuation that rivals some of the world's largest public companies?
verdict: Anthropic is now worth [$380 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html)—more than most Fortune 500 companies—based on [$14 billion in annualized revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) and [10x annual growth](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). [It's raised $67.3 billion total](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors) and is preparing for an IPO that betting markets suggest could happen before OpenAI. The story isn't whether Anthropic is growing—it is. The story is whether [80% enterprise customer concentration](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) can sustain 10x growth forever, and whether the company can turn revenue into profit before the market decides it's paid enough. If you're in tech stocks, [Azure](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/), or [AI ETFs](https://kraneshares.com/will-anthropic-or-xai-ipo-in-2026/), you're already betting on the answer.

content.headline: Anthropic Just Became a $380 Billion Unicorn—And Nobody Can Agree If It's Worth It
content.summary: Anthropic closed a [$30 billion funding round](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) at a [$380 billion valuation](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) on February 12, 2026—the second-largest private tech raise ever. Now the real question: Is the AI startup's [$14 billion annualized revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) enough to justify a valuation that rivals some of the world's largest public companies?
content.analysis:
## The Headlines

Anthropric just closed a [$30 billion funding round](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) at a [$380 billion post-money valuation](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html). That makes it [the second-largest private financing round in tech history](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html)—beaten only by [OpenAI's $40 billion raise in 2025](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

To put that in perspective: Anthropic is now worth more than most Fortune 500 companies. And it's still private.

But here's the catch. The company has raised [approximately $67.3 billion total over 17 rounds from 90 investors since its founding in 2021](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors). That's a lot of money chasing a company that's burning a lot of money to build AI. So everyone wants a piece—but not everyone agrees they should.

## The Backstory

Anthropric was [founded in 2021 by former OpenAI researchers, including CEO Dario Amodei and President Daniela Amodei](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html). It's the company behind Claude, an AI assistant that's gained serious traction.

But traction doesn't mean sustainable yet. Here's what we know about the money:

[Amazon became a minority stakeholder with an initial $1.25 billion investment in September 2023](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html), then [invested the remaining $2.75 billion in March 2024](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html). [In November 2024, Amazon invested another $4 billion, bringing its total to $8 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html). [AWS is Anthropic's primary cloud and training partner](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

[Google's total investment in Anthropic now exceeds $3 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html). [Court documents revealed Google owns a 14% stake and planned an additional $750 million via convertible debt](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-owns-14-percent-of-generative-ai-business-anthropic/)—though [Google is capped at 15% ownership with no voting rights or board seats](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-owns-14-percent-of-generative-ai-business-anthropic/).

Then came November 2025. [Microsoft committed to invest up to $5 billion as part of a partnership deal, with Anthropic committing to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/). [Nvidia committed to invest up to $10 billion and establish a deep technology collaboration to optimize Anthropic models for Nvidia architectures](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/).

The February 2026 round that just closed included [GIC (Singapore's sovereign wealth fund) and Coatue Management as leaders](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/anthropic-finalizes-30-billion-funding-at-380-billion-value), with [D.E. Shaw & Co., Dragoneer Investment Group, Peter Thiel's Founders Fund, Iconiq, and MGX co-leading](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/anthropic-finalizes-30-billion-funding-at-380-billion-value). [Sequoia Capital, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Nvidia, and Microsoft also participated](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/anthropic-finalizes-30-billion-funding-at-380-billion-value).

## The Takes

So what does all this money buy you? Revenue—and plenty of skeptics.

[Anthropic's annualized revenue run rate has reached $14 billion, growing more than 10x annually over the past three years](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). [Claude Code alone has run-rate revenue above $2.5 billion](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). [Anthropic gets approximately 80% of its business from enterprises](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

That's bullish. Enterprise customers are typically stickier than consumers, and 10x annual growth is explosive by any standard.

But here's the bull case from the investors themselves: they're betting on two things. First, that AI will become as central to the economy as electricity or the internet. Second, that Anthropic's Claude will be the platform that powers that shift. [According to Ramp data, 1 in 5 businesses using Ramp now pay for Anthropic, up from 1 in 25 a year ago. About 79% of OpenAI users also pay for Anthropic](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation).

That's adoption velocity. It suggests Claude isn't just a curiosity—people are willing to pay for it *alongside* OpenAI's ChatGPT.

Now for the cautious take (also from the source material): [Claude Code's popularity and Anthropic's productivity tools have contributed to a selloff in software stocks, with the sector losing around $2 trillion in market capitalization from its peak](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

That's not a criticism of Anthropic—it's a fact about market rotation. Investors are rotating *out* of traditional software into AI. Whether that's rational is the billion-dollar question nobody can answer yet.

## Real Talk

Let's connect the dots.

Anthropric is burning capital at a massive scale—you don't get $30 billion raised in a single round without needing it. [The company has raised $67.3 billion total since 2021](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors). That's eight rounds in five years. That's not "normal startup growth"—that's a cash furnace.

But the revenue is real. [$14 billion in annualized run rate](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) is not fake money. Neither is [80% enterprise customer concentration](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

Here's what's actually interesting: Anthropic is preparing for an IPO. [The company hired law firm Wilson Sonsini to prepare for a potential IPO that could take place as early as 2026, according to the Financial Times](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-plans-ipo-early-2026-004854547.html). [A betting platform shows Anthropic at a 72% probability of IPOing before OpenAI](https://kraneshares.com/will-anthropic-or-xai-ipo-in-2026/).

That's the real story: the market is betting Anthropic goes public *soon*. Which means all this private capital raising isn't about staying private—it's about building momentum for a public market debut. If you're a late-stage VC or a sovereign wealth fund, you're buying at [$380 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) hoping the IPO values it higher.

The question becomes: Can Anthropic maintain this revenue growth *and* profitability? Because right now, [$14 billion in revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) against [$67 billion raised lifetime](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors) means the math is still theoretical.

## The Bottom Line

Anthropic just became a [$380 billion company](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) based on [$14 billion in annualized revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). That values it at roughly 27x revenue—which is rich for any company, let alone one still in growth mode.

But [it's also growing 10x annually](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). And [80% of its revenue comes from enterprise customers](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html), not fickle consumers.

If you own tech stocks, AI ETFs like [AGIX (which holds a 4.21% portfolio position in Anthropic as of Dec 31, 2025, and is reportedly the only publicly listed ETF with direct Anthropic equity exposure)](https://kraneshares.com/will-anthropic-or-xai-ipo-in-2026/), or shares in [Amazon](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html), [Google](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html), [Microsoft](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/), or [Nvidia](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/)—you're already exposed to this bet.

The real question: Is Anthropic's valuation the beginning of a new AI era, or the peak of the hype cycle? The sources show the capital believes it's the former. Whether you agree is up to you.
risks: [Revenue is high but profitability is unproven—$14 billion in annualized run rate against $67.3 billion raised lifetime means losses are still substantial](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation).

[Claude Code and AI productivity tools have contributed to a $2 trillion selloff in software stocks, suggesting Anthropic's growth may cannibalize existing software revenues](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

[80% of revenue is enterprise-concentrated, creating customer concentration risk if a major buyer reduces spending](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html).

[IPO timing and valuation are uncertain—the market has priced in an IPO as early as 2026, but a delayed or lower-valued debut could disappoint late-stage investors](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-plans-ipo-early-2026-004854547.html).
catalysts: [IPO as early as 2026 with law firm Wilson Sonsini hired to prepare, backed by betting market showing 72% probability of going public before OpenAI](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-plans-ipo-early-2026-004854547.html).

[Enterprise adoption accelerating—1 in 5 Ramp users now pay for Anthropic, up from 1 in 25 a year ago, and 79% of OpenAI users also subscribe to Claude](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation).

[Strategic partnerships with Azure and Nvidia providing deep integration and competitive moat—Anthropic committed to $30 billion in Azure compute spending](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/).

[Continued 10x annual revenue growth could accelerate path to profitability and justify current $380 billion valuation if sustained](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation).

dataPoints.label: OpenAI Users Also Paying for Anthropic

content.finalVerdict: Anthropic is now worth [$380 billion](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html)—more than most Fortune 500 companies—based on [$14 billion in annualized revenue](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation) and [10x annual growth](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-raises-30b-at-380b-valuation). [It's raised $67.3 billion total](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors) and is preparing for an IPO that betting markets suggest could happen before OpenAI. The story isn't whether Anthropic is growing—it is. The story is whether [80% enterprise customer concentration](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-closes-30-billion-funding-round-at-380-billion-valuation.html) can sustain 10x growth forever, and whether the company can turn revenue into profit before the market decides it's paid enough. If you're in tech stocks, [Azure](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/11/18/microsoft-nvidia-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships/), or [AI ETFs](https://kraneshares.com/will-anthropic-or-xai-ipo-in-2026/), you're already betting on the answer.
